personas.name: Reviewer
issues: Curriculum lacks clear progression from simple to complex concepts
No explicit connection to real-world examples beyond classroom
Assessment criteria not clearly communicated to parents
Limited differentiation for diverse learning needs
No guidance for parents on how to support at home

personas.name: Questioner
issues: Why is 90% the target rather than 100% for basic living/non-living区分?
What prerequisite knowledge is assumed?
How does this connect to next grade's standards?
What evidence shows this 90% target is appropriate?
Why are sentence stems the primary assessment method?

personas.name: Comparer
issues: Compared to common core standards, this seems basic
Kindergarten already covers living/non-living in many districts
No cross-curricular connections (math counting, art drawing)
Lacks STEM integration opportunities
International curricula often introduce classification earlier

personas.name: Critic
issues: 90% accuracy target is too low for such a fundamental concept
Exit ticket drawing task is subjective and hard to assess
No consideration for regional/cultural contexts (e.g., desert vs rainforest ecosystems)
Biased toward animal-centric examples, plant examples underrepresented
No safety or ethical considerations mentioned
Curriculum assumes homogeneous classroom without addressing disabilities or neurodiversity
